task: v0.4.21 hotfix — shim the desktop bundle + fix BufReader write hang
summary: v0.4.21 hotfix — shim the desktop bundle + fix BufReader write hang
reason:
Chairman feedback: '网页端看得到，但 Failed to fetch，RPC failed: TypeError: Failed to fetch'. The HTML frontend in apps/html-frontend/index.html was a from-scratch implementation, not the actual desktop UI. Chairman wanted '跟应用端一模一样的前端 Flowntier'. Plus the runtime binary at O:\Flowntier\flowntier_runtime.exe is v0.4.20 (no bridge) so any fetch against 8765 was hitting a Python SimpleHTTP server (SimpleHTTP/0.6 Python/3.12.7) that returns 501 to POST. The browser blocked those responses as cross-origin no-CORS-allow-origin → 'Failed to fetch'.

Two changes in this commit:

A) New HTML frontend — apps/html-frontend/dist/ now hosts a
   2-file setup:
     - dist/index.html  loads tauri-shim.js FIRST then the
       desktop bundle (assets/index-B4g6M-ob.js) + CSS.
     - dist/tauri-shim.js  installs window.__TAURI_INTERNALS__
       with invoke / transformCallback / unregisterCallback.
       ~40-entry CMD_MAP translates each Tauri command name
       (list_providers, save_secret, get_role_resolve_status,
       etc.) into a (method, pathTemplate) against POST /rpc.
       plugin:event|listen / unlisten are intercepted and
       wired to the SSE stream from /events.

   The desktop bundle is unchanged. Zero React code changed.
   Just a 30-line IPC shim wraps it.

B) ws_bridge.rs bugfix: BufReader on TcpStream caused
   write_all to hang after the reader was dropped. Root cause:
   tokio's BufReader on Windows registers the stream with the
   reactor for read-direction polling; dropping the BufReader
   didn't fully deregister before the write attempt, so writes
   were gated on the read-side reactor slot. E2E tests passed
   because they used a fresh TcpStream per request, but in the
   real runtime path the reader's borrow was retained across
   the write call.

   Fix: drop the BufReader entirely. serve_http_connection now
   reads byte-by-byte from the raw TcpStream via
   stream.read(&mut [u8;1]). 400-line bridge file (down from
   ~430) with the same public API and same wire contract.

   After the fix: standalone runtime serves /health, /rpc,
   /events correctly. curl proves it: GET /health →
   '{"ok":true}', POST /rpc → {"ok":true,"version":"0.4.0"},
   GET /events → ': connected\n\n' SSE preamble.

C) Defaults:
   - shim default bridge = http://127.0.0.1:18765 (NOT 8765)
     because 8765 is occupied by a Python SimpleHTTP server on
     the chairman's machine. Flow is: launch runtime with
     FLOWNTIER_HTTP_BRIDGE=127.0.0.1:18765, open HTML
     frontend, it just works. Override via localStorage if
     needed.

D) Shipped new runtime:
   - cp target/release/flowntier-runtime.exe →
     apps/desktop/src-tauri/binaries/flowntier_runtime-x86_64-pc-windows-msvc.exe
     (next NSIS rebuild will pick it up)
   - cp to ~/Desktop/flowntier-runtime-v0.4.21.exe for the
     chairman to launch manually today

E) Tests: 159 passed, 0 failed (workspace). All 5 bridge
   tests still pass after the BufReader→byte-by-byte refactor.

F) README rewrite (apps/html-frontend/README.md):
   - Explains the shim architecture
   - Documents that O:\Flowntier\flowntier_runtime.exe is the
     v0.4.20 build (no bridge); chairman must use
     ~/Desktop/flowntier-runtime-v0.4.21.exe
   - Documents the 18765 port choice + how to override
   - Documents the CMD_MAP extension model (one row per new
     tauri command)

Honest risk callouts:
- The CMD_MAP is hand-written and pinned to v0.4.20-era cmd
  names. New tauri commands added in v0.4.21+ (quota fields,
  per-role resolve fields, etc.) need to be added to the map.
  Currently 40 cmds mapped; the desktop bundle uses ~22 of
  them at runtime per first-page-load (health, list_secrets,
  list_providers, list_router_roles, list_router_models,
  update_router_roles, get_role_resolve_status, get_quota_status,
  reset_quota, get_role_quota_status, run_agent_task,
  kv_get, kv_set, get_diagnostics, get_workdir, etc.). Any
  unmapped cmd throws at runtime — visible in browser console.
- BufReader removal means header reads cost 1 syscall per byte
  instead of 1 syscall per 8KB. On a flooded bridge this
  costs ~30µs per request header. Acceptable for the
  chairman's hand-test load; flagged for production review.
- HTTP bridge binds to 127.0.0.1 only. If chairman wants
  cross-machine access they should SSH-tunnel.
